capture_method: Manual local replay against the committed routing fixture and current SKILL.md contract.

observed_output_sections.heading: which route or flow was checked
observed_output_sections.content: Start with only the failing route and the one button tap that reproduces the blank page so the run stays narrow and the result is easy to attribute.

observed_output_sections.heading: whether automation really connected
observed_output_sections.content: Treat the host DevTools session as part of the test: confirm the checker connected, the selected route opened, and a report was written before drawing any runtime conclusion.

observed_output_sections.heading: what runtime evidence was collected
observed_output_sections.content: Use page path, primary selector presence, console output, exception events, and the generated report as the primary signals; screenshots are best-effort only.

observed_output_sections.heading: whether the issue is in repo code, session state, a local service dependency, or still needs manual visual confirmation
observed_output_sections.content: A selector/assertion failure or runtime exception points back to repo behavior, an automation connection problem points to DevTools state, a route-level `request:fail` points to a local service dependency, and a screenshot-only mismatch still needs manual visual confirmation.

observed_output_sections.heading: the next command or user action
observed_output_sections.content: Run `node tools/wechat-gui-check/check.js --config <route-config-path> --project-path <project-root> --route <route-key>` and inspect the generated report before widening the route coverage.

notes: Captured as a replayable prompt-and-answer sample. This is transcript-backed local evidence, not a live installed-skill router transcript.
